item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The ant eating the mouse is basically how most vore works!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is weird biology and I love it! I also appreciate the scientific details combined with the anomalous explanation for deviations. +1

item.title: Re: Amnesiacs?
item.content:encoded: "Amnestics" is the correct term to use, but since the site originally used "amnesiacs" as the term for "things the Foundation uses to erase memories", we allow authors to use it as a stylistic choice. Older articles remain as they are unless someone gets the author's permission to make the change.

item.title: Amnesiacs?
item.content:encoded: Should be "amnestics," no? This occurs twice in the text.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: upvoted, thought it would be like petshop of horrors, but its different :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upvoted at the piranha hamsters. They're just too cute.

so maybe that's where Nibbles came from

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm wondering what would happen if you placed an order for some sort of mythical or extinct animal. Unicorn or Velociraptor, what happen then?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wonder what would happen if we took a photo of these critters with SCP-978. Does a hamstiranha think of itself as a hamster, or a piranha?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This reminds me (in a good way) of the old "custom pet shop" Monty Python skit.

I second the request for a secondary experiment log for others to add to:

"Requested: SCP-682
Recieved: Sea Cucumber"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As a computer scientist/game developer, I envision this as someone using the code for one animal and the art assets for another. This amuses me greatly. +1

Also please add an experiment log page for people to contribute their own.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That works too.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Or they simply do not know how to animals.

I actually think that what's making these is some thing that understands what the various types of animal are — that is, how they behave — and knows what some living things look like, but has no idea how the two go together. So it gets an order, thinks of an animal, goes, "Yup, that looks like a guinea pig to me," and creates this mixed-up abomination out of the ether.

It's just trying to make people happy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I want my god damn guinea piranhas and I want them now! I will pay for them with this upvote right here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm very gratified that it's not related to Wondertainment or any other GoI,

If there was a GoI whose philosophy was "we make weird and absurd things because weird and absurd things are neat", I likely would have credited it to them. While I sort of get that vibe from Wondertainment, the much bigger vibe I get from them is that they're simply a toy company which unlike other, lesser toy companies doesn't let mere trifles like "physics" and "logic" get in the way of making the best product they can.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
To bring in another voice, I totally disagree. The premise is awesome outside of the fact that it is possible to have rotating shifts watching the door, but I guess maybe the box would appear when someone blinks.

Also, the ant one is problematic because I can't even pretend to imagine an ant devouring a mouse. I don't even know what an ant's mouth looks like, or what its body would look like while digesting. Sounds like it would look like a black water balloon with tiny body parts on either side.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yay! I made a good thing happen! Kinda!

Also, the thing that won the upvote for me was the ant eating the mouse. That's just fucking disturbing and brilliant.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Damnit!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Instead of upvote package contained lynx.

Would not buy again.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i'll just leave this here

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Who mails a bobcat a lynx an upvote?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The language in this is at times convoluted (especially the paragraph describing the animal's behavior), and the test logs deviate from tone, but overall I think this is amusing. I'm very gratified that it's not related to Wondertainment or any other GoI, though I can't help but think that this might just potentially be the semantic dissociation zone at work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Also, the inspiration for this was Scantron's comment about "seeing eye hornets" in discussion section of The Big List Of Overdone SCP Cliches page, so that might be where the sense of familiarity is coming from.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I could swear I've seen this concept before, but I can't think of where.

Well, while I was writing it it did sort of remind me of SCP-1539, the Semantic Dissociator, and there's also a few animal hybridization SCPs, like SCP-889.

By the way, does it do anything if we mention "human"? Or if we mention an extinct species, like "Stegosaurus" or "dodo bird"?

Those get no response. I'll think about clearing that up directly, but I feel like adding any more that isn't about the individual animals would clutter the article up too much.

Also, in my original conception of the article, the Foundation mentioned "mayfly" and got a human baby which grew old and died over the course of a single day.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Anomalous pets….for free??? Yes. My upvote will be arriving on your doorstep.

Although, I would write a letter of complaint about the dead rat. That deserves a replacement.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ahahaha, what? Confusing and strange, but… I kinda like it. I want a hamirsanha now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is excellent.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I could swear I've seen this concept before, but I can't think of where. I'm going to mull it over a bit before I vote one way or another.

By the way, does it do anything if we mention "human"? Or if we mention an extinct species, like "Stegosaurus" or "dodo bird"?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The mixed-up animals are the point of the article, with the premise merely being there as a source and to tie them together, so that's okay.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Premise nothing much ,but the descriptions of mixups are sweet.

Animal Mentioned: Eurasian lynx.
Animal Received: An upvote.
